projectDescription: The Ethereum Name Service (ENS) is a decentralized naming protocol on Ethereum that maps human-readable names like alice.eth to blockchain addresses, content hashes, and arbitrary metadata.
keyFeatures: Human-readable .eth names mapped to Ethereum addresses and other blockchain addresses
DNS name integration via DNSSEC — import existing DNS names into ENS
Decentralized Web (dWeb) content hosting via IPFS/IPNS/Swarm content hashes
Primary names (reverse resolution) — map an Ethereum address back to an ENS name
Text records — attach arbitrary metadata (Twitter, GitHub, email, URL) to any ENS name
Multichain address records — store addresses for 100+ blockchains on one ENS name
Subdomains — create hierarchical names and build custom subname registrars
Name Wrapper — wrap ENS names as ERC-1155 NFTs with fine-grained permission fuses
CCIP-Read (EIP-3668) — trustless off-chain and L2 name resolution
ENSv2 — next-generation protocol with native L2 support and hierarchical registries
Sign-In with Ethereum (SIWE) — ENS-powered decentralized authentication
ENS DAO governance — $ENS token voting on protocol upgrades and treasury
ENS Improvement Proposals (ENSIPs) — community-driven standards process
publicApiSurface: ENS Registry — owner(), resolver(), ttl(), setOwner(), setResolver(), setTTL(), setSubnodeOwner(), setSubnodeRecord()
ETH Registrar — available(), rentPrice(), register(), renew(), reclaim()
Public Resolver — addr(), setAddr(), text(), setText(), contenthash(), setContenthash(), pubkey(), setPubkey()
Name Wrapper — wrapETH2LD(), wrap(), unwrap(), setFuses(), setChildFuses(), setSubnodeRecord()
wagmi hooks — useEnsName(), useEnsAvatar(), useEnsAddress(), useEnsResolver(), useEnsText()
ensjs library — getName(), getAddress(), getTextRecord(), getContentHashRecord(), getResolver()
CCIP-Read / EIP-3668 — offchain data gateway callback interface
Subgraph — GraphQL queries for domains, registrations, resolvers, accounts
